mapper.sql: select d.dept_id, d.parent_id, d.ancestors, d.dept_name, d.dept_full_path, d.dept_category, d.order_num, d.leader, d.phone, d.email, d.status, d.is_deleted, d.create_by, d.create_time, (select count(1) from sys_user u where u.dept_id = d.dept_id and u.is_deleted = '0') as user_count from sys_dept d
mapper.select: select count(1) from sys_user where dept_id = #{deptId} and is_deleted = '0'
select count(1) from sys_dept where is_deleted = '0' and parent_id = #{deptId} limit 1
select * from sys_dept where find_in_set(#{deptId}, ancestors)
select count(*) from sys_dept where status = 0 and is_deleted = '0' and find_in_set(#{deptId}, ancestors)
where dept_name=#{deptName} and parent_id = #{parentId} and is_deleted = '0' limit 1
mapper.insert:
SELECT LAST_INSERT_ID()
insert into sys_dept( dept_id, parent_id, dept_name, dept_full_path, dept_category, ancestors, order_num, leader, phone, email, status, create_by, create_time )values( #{deptId}, #{parentId}, #{deptName}, #{deptFullPath}, #{deptCategory}, #{ancestors}, #{orderNum}, #{leader}, #{phone}, #{email}, #{status}, #{createBy}, sysdate() )
mapper.update:
update sys_dept set status = '0' where dept_id in
#{deptId}
update sys_dept set status = #{status}, update_by = #{updateBy}, update_time = sysdate() where dept_id = #{deptId}
mapper.delete: update sys_dept set is_deleted = '1' where dept_id = #{deptId}
mapper.update: update sys_dept set ancestors = #{ancestors} where dept_id = #{deptId}
